records.applies_when: a feature ships instruction text, docs, or installed blocks that name CLI flags or behavior owned by a later feature
records.lesson: Check installed instruction/doc artifacts against the ACTUAL CLI surface at reconcile - a proved feature's shipped text can advertise a sibling's unbuilt flag, putting doc reality ahead of implementation.

records.applies_when: wiring an init/setup command to a doctor or diagnostic path that can mutate derived state (drain queues, rebuild indexes)
records.lesson: Setup/init verification must be non-mutating - route init through a read-only diagnosis mode; draining/rebuilding belongs to explicit commands, or idempotence criteria silently break as later features extend the diagnostic.

records.applies_when: a success criterion asserts an exact invariant (exactly one commit, exactly N artifacts) over an operation with content- or time-dependent no-op edge cases
records.lesson: Exact-invariant criteria need the no-op edge named and handled - an operation that changes nothing must either report itself as a no-op or be made impossible; tests that sleep past the boundary hide the hole.

records.applies_when: a CLI calls a local model daemon with a client-requested context size (num_ctx) under a strict latency budget
records.lesson: A client num_ctx differing from the daemon's loaded context forces a model RELOAD that dwarfs steady-state latency AND is environment-variable (0.8-3.8s measured for the same model) - no fixed budget under a tight NFR reliably covers it. Design the contract as graceful degradation + self-warm (the timed-out request loads the model server-side; the next call succeeds warm) and pin keep_alive in deployment to make the window once-per-boot.
records.source: BUILD_LOG.md wave 4 walkthrough finding + wave 6 checkpoint finding 4 re-measurement (.kodos/evidence/walkthrough/wave6-f36-postfix.txt)

records.applies_when: a live retest proves a timing/performance fix and its pass depends on ambient environment state (page cache, daemon warmth, loaded models)
records.lesson: Record the environmental precondition (cache warmth, daemon/model state) as part of any live timing-proof evidence, and state the claim as the guaranteed contract (what happens when the budget is exceeded), not the best-case observation - an environment-dependent pass journaled as 'now succeeds' becomes a false claim the next time the environment differs.
records.source: BUILD_LOG.md wave 6 checkpoint finding 4: wave-4 'first search now succeeds' passed page-cache-warm only; cold re-measurement 3.83s failed the same budget

records.applies_when: adjudicating a fresh-eyes reviewer's drift finding that attributes a claim to the wrong artifact or protocol
records.lesson: Before remediating a drift finding, re-read the ledger/spec text it cites - a directionally-correct finding can misattribute the defect (e.g. blaming evidence when a journal sentence imported a sibling feature's protocol clause), and the cheap fix differs by an order of magnitude from the expensive one (one-line correction vs re-running an observation).
records.source: BUILD_LOG.md wave 6 checkpoint finding 2 (verifier-ca8z): F11's 'prompt never mentions memory' clause wrongly imported into the F10 journal entry
